symbols: imperial crown — supreme temporal authority in Christendom
scepter — sovereign right to rule and dispense justice
orb — dominion over the earthly realm, surmounted by the cross signifying Christ's lordship over all
golden armor — military might and the emperor's role as defender of the faith
red brocade — imperial dignity and the blood-sanctioned authority of the throne
description: A half-length portrait of Emperor Maximilian I in three-quarter view, facing right. He wears a resplendent golden suit of armor beneath a richly jeweled ceremonial collar or chain. On his head rests an elaborate imperial crown. In his right hand he holds a scepter, and in his left a small orb surmounted by a cross. The background is divided between a rich red brocade curtain on the left and a dark opening on the right. His long brown hair falls to his shoulders. The painting has a flat, iconic quality characteristic of late fifteenth-century court portraiture.
